testTextPlaceholder: Paste or type text to test your regex...
flagGlobalDesc: Find all matches rather than stopping after the first match
flagMultilineDesc: Treat beginning and end characters (^ and $) as working over multiple lines
flagDotAllDesc: Make . match newline characters as well
flagUnicodeDesc: Treat pattern and input as Unicode sequences
flagStickyDesc: Match only from the index indicated by the lastIndex property
flagHasIndicesDesc: Generate indices for each capture group in match results
emptyPattern: Enter a regex pattern to get started
emptyInput: Paste or type text to test
replaceWithoutG: replaced {count} (no g flag, only first match)
patternMaybeRedos: Pattern took too long — possible catastrophic backtracking
html5SpecEmail: Follows HTML5 spec, not strict RFC 5322. May reject some valid emails.
simplifiedIPv6: Simplified form — does not support :: compression notation
presetStrongPasswordDesc: Min 8 chars, uppercase, lowercase, digit, special character
cheatsheetLazyVariant: Lazy variant (match as few as possible)

descriptions.whatIsP1: A regex (regular expression) tester is an interactive tool that lets you build and test regex patterns against sample text in real time.
descriptions.whatIsP2: As you type, matches are highlighted directly in the test text, and a detailed match list shows each match with its position, captured groups, and length.
descriptions.whatIsP3: This tool runs entirely in your browser — no data is ever sent to any server.
descriptions.featuresP1: Real-time match highlighting — see matches visually in your test text with alternating colors for clarity.
descriptions.featuresP2: Capture group inspection — view named and numbered capture groups for each match.
descriptions.featuresP3: Replacement preview — test regex-based find-and-replace with support for $1, $&, and '$<name>' replacement tokens.
descriptions.featuresP4: Pattern explanation — token-level breakdown of your regex, showing what each part means (e.g., \d+ → "one or more digits").
descriptions.featuresP5: Catastrophic backtracking protection — matching runs in a background thread with a timeout, so bad patterns won't freeze the page.
descriptions.featuresP6: Preset library — quick-start with common patterns for email, URL, IP, phone, passwords, and more.
descriptions.useCasesP1: Learning regex — see how each token contributes to matching, and test your understanding against sample text.
descriptions.useCasesP2: Debugging — paste an existing regex and test it against actual data to verify it matches what you expect.
descriptions.useCasesP3: Data extraction — use capture groups to extract specific parts from log files, CSVs, or structured text.
descriptions.useCasesP4: Find and replace — preview complex text transformations before applying them in your code.
descriptions.useCasesP5: Validation — test email, phone, URL, and other validation patterns against edge cases.
descriptions.limitationsP1: JavaScript regex only — this tool uses the browser's built-in regex engine. PCRE, Python, and other regex flavors may behave differently.
descriptions.limitationsP2: No lookbehind in older browsers — lookbehind assertions (?<=...) require ES2018+. Safari only added support in 2023.
descriptions.limitationsP3: Match cap at 1,000 — to keep the UI responsive, only the first 1,000 matches are rendered.
descriptions.limitationsP4: Capturing groups not highlighted in text — groups are shown in the match list, not inline in the highlighted text.
